entry.title: Re: Production Schedule Profile field is not visible in P...
entry.content:
Please use the Adapt UI in the app Manage Product Master Data to add the field Prod. Sched. Profile

 

Please refer to the resource Adapt UI that explains how to add the field and list of fields available

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps/Process Automation: "Workflow Definiti...
entry.content:
Hello all, 

The solution provided is correct. 

SAP Note contained one dot, reason why was not being displayed/found correctly. 

Here is the correct link of the SAP Note

If one follows the instructions described to include an API Key in the header it will work. Or deploying the Project in the Public Environment. 

Probably everyone gets confused when the deployment action is being performed because the "Public" enviroment is market for deprecated and people deploy into another environment instead. 

To access that environment, it requires the API Key. 

Hope it helps. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: API for add new Pricing Conditions to an Existing Pur...
entry.content: Hi Jagoda, the problem is not in the pricing and values. That's a technical problem because API cannot support 'POST' Operation

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: you didn't answer the first question? we know what you want, first we have to check what you have now, then we will try to figure out getting what you want

entry.title: Cannot change or update the 'Without Qty Posting' field in the Return Request document.
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m encountering an issue in SAP Business One version 10 FP 2502 and FP 2505. When creating a Return Request document, I’m able to check or uncheck the Without Qty Posting option. However, once the Return Request is saved, the system no longer allows me to update this field—I cannot check or uncheck it anymore.

It’s important to note that the Return Request document is not based on another document; it was created independently. I tested this same scenario in earlier versions, and in those cases I was able to modify the Without Qty Posting checkbox after saving.

 
Is there any setting or option available in these versions that could help resolve this?

entry.title: Re: Calibration: People Analytics does not return all the sessions
entry.content: I would check Query preview first to see if it extracts the data on the third session at all

entry.title: Re: Sales return generating multiple Nota Fiscal
entry.content: Thank you. I am going to review the configuration

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
first check the ui5 version.

when preview, what the version is?

when run in flp, what the ui5 version is?

entry.title: ECC to BPC
entry.content:
Dear All,

We aim to establish a direct mapping of GL accounts from ECC to BPC (Consolidations). Could you please specify which fields within the GL master record indicate that an account is configured to flow into BPC? Our goal is to identify the relevant account attributes in ECC GL Master that determine whether an account is included in BPC.

Current process: ECC - Anaplan - BPC

Anaplan is converting the GL data apser BPC requirement and we are thinking to get rid of Anaplan 

Below alternative way we tried but not suits our requirements:

Custom table replace to Anaplan

Alternative account number in GL master

Group COA

Thank

entry.title: Re: xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException Error while loading SAP...
entry.content: Thank you, Don. My application is a legacy C++/MFC MDI application that heavily relies on DAO and Crystal Reports 8.5. Unfortunately, due to architectural constraints and dependencies, it cannot be migrated to 64-bit at this time. Given this, I'm currently limited to using the 32-bit Crystal Reports runtime, and I understand that support for it is expected to end in December 2025. I'm exploring options for maintaining compatibility in the short term while planning a longer-term migration strategy. Any suggestions or shared experiences from others in a similar situation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Partially compounded display keys in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Hi @ravidevadig15, Not yet... The investigation is still ongoing. I will share the results once it is solved.

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: The answer to the first question. The order is saving using system time zone, which is CST.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors Hands-On Practice Systems
entry.content: Are there also SuccessFactors practice systems planned with SAP Joule?

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
then why can't you figure out what's wrong?

you look at cst time, and feed the system(from fiori) using your india local time, how that will work???

entry.title: Re: ATC - How to Implement AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content: Thank you very much Laura. It worked for me. Regards

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
Thanks for the clarification.

I would check the following:

1) I'm not so sure that in this scenario you would need to fill the ICF path in App manager (in red below) as you have already the Component ID (in green below). As this is an easy change, I'd try clearing the ICF path field inside the technical catalog in App manager and testing it again. If you have used references in your business catalogs there is no change needed on them.

2) It's possible to see that you have create a different combination of semantic object + action in your adapted version of the app - which I believe it's a good idea.
Standard app: #MaintenanceJob-performJob
Adapted custom app: #MaintenanceJobz-zperformJobTest

Can you confirm that when  testing in your S/4HANA system (not in BAS) your user has access only to the Adapted custom app? 

If you have used the same semantic object + action I bet that the launchpad could lead to inconsistencies but it's another test worth doing. In order to achieve that you cannot have both apps in the same catalog. You might want to delete the reference for the standard app from your business catalog to test that. Make sure your user do not have access to the standard app by means of another catalog.

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
Hi @junwu ! So in the MIME report in SE80 we have 1.108, preview is 1.136, and version in FLP with the app variant is 1.108. When I created the project I chose the latest version instead of the system version (1.108).

Should I have chosen the system version? This is new for me so I figured latest would be best.

Please let me know!

Regards, Patrick

entry.title: Introducing: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private featuring 2023 FPS03
entry.content:
Stop Avoiding Releases & Embrace Cloud Innovation

We acknowledge that upgrading ERP technology presents a complex and time-intensive challenge. It is inherent that ensuring seamless integration of new innovations with existing technical APIs and third-party software requires diligent preparation. Unlike the swift upgrades associated with cellular devices, ERP Private technology necessitates a more intricate approach. Otherwise, organizations utilizing ERP Private solutions would readily adopt the latest innovations upon releases.

In response to these challenges, SAP Enterprise Support has introduced the release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private. This comprehensive digital library offers up-to-date release information and enablement content, strategically organized for ease of access. The release navigator provides valuable guidance on release timelines, introducing insights into new feature enablement, and supports the change management process. By staying informed on upcoming releases, organizations are better equipped to navigate the complexities of ERP Private software upgrades. Take a look today!

 

Fictional Use Case

Situation #1: Globex, a fictional multinational enterprise, operates across several industries such as manufacturing, supply chain, and procurement, leveraging distinct lines of business (LoBs). To manage its complex operations, Globex has decided to implement SAP Cloud ERP Private. As the enterprise anticipates an upcoming system upgrade, it is imperative for Globex to access detailed release information and enablement content associated with the new features. This proactive approach will mitigate potential system disruptions and ensure that the organization fully capitalizes on the benefits of the latest innovations across its operations.

 

Resolution #1: By leveraging the release navigator, Globex efficiently accesses the Release Calendar located on the Product Specific page (see Figure 1: Product Specific page) to track upcoming product releases. Additionally, Globex benefits from curated release content and enablement resources, specifically tailored for manufacturing, supply chain, and procurement lines, conveniently organized under the Lines of Business tab. This streamlined approach accelerates the process of locating and scaling the necessary resources to comprehend the features being adopted.

 Figure 1: Product Specific page

 

Situation #2: Globex needs to be able to point new employee's to the latest information on the tool and does not have time to walk them through how to use it.

Resolution #2: Globex can access integrated content and enablement features directly in the release navigator, providing valuable resources for their organization. Each main page features a blue light bulb icon that users can select to access additional information and a guided tour of that page. Furthermore, new employees can be directed to the Get Assistance page located within the side menu (refer to Figure 2: Get Assistance page). This page includes a link to a curated blog tag within the SAP Community, offering access to a collection of blogs specifically related to the release navigator. Additionally, new employees will have access to a authenticated version of the tool that supports over 40 languages for translation. The translation feature can be activated via the shell bar, located at the top right corner of each page.

Figure 2: Get Assistance page

 

Connect with Us

We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback for specific content requests for future updates to the release navigator. Please feel free to contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post. You can also leave your feedback directly in the release navigator by selecting the User Feedback inside the side menu (see Figure 3: User Feedback page).

 Figure 3: User Feedback page

 

Always Bring Your Best,

Andrew Ford

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content: that's probably the problem, switch your bas to lower version to see if the problem is reproduced, very likely the function you are expecting is not implemented in lower version.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry Regarding Email Storage Location in S4 Client...
entry.content:
Email addresses are stored in ADR6 (whether this is universal I cannot confirm).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content: If you deploy your app in the S/4HANA system (which I guess is the only way possible in your scenario as it's an adaptation project) the SAPUI5 version used when you open the app is always the SAPUI5 version available in the S/4HANA system (1.108 as you are in S/4HANA 2022). If you would deploy your app in BTP (which again, I don't think it's possible for adaptations) you can use any SAPUI5 version you want which is available in SAP's CDN. It's true that when you preview your app from BAS, it uses the latest SAPUI5 version by default. You can test in different versions in BAS by changing your ui5.yaml file or by using run configurations. But again, in this scenario you are stuck on 1.108. Testing in BAS using this version helps understanding where the issue is but it will not fix the issue unless the bug is in SAPUI5 itself. In that is the case the best you can do is upgrading SAPUI5 patch to the most recent one avaialble 1.108.44 is the latest) See https://ui5.sap.com/versionoverview.html

entry.title: Re: HTML5 app not showing in BTP Cockpit After deployed
entry.content:
Hello!

Please set this parameter to true and run again, maybe it solves the issue: 

HTML5Runtime_enabled: true

If it doesn't work, I would recommend deleting the mta.yaml file and generate it again.

entry.title: Partial Refund process in FICA
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone please help me on Partial refund process for FICA customer?

if there is a credit of $120.00- on account and out of this only $100.00 needs to be refunded .is there any way user can select to refund only $100.00?

Manual refund: Customer has a credit on account due to overpayment. But before requesting the refund, that credit is cleared new invoice. In this case a debit entry is posted to customer account, and the document number is sent to accounting to create a manual check.

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
You guys are awesome with your responses.

So, it looks like the versions are the issue... I changed the YAML to use 1.108.38 (system version), the field rearrangement does not work, then I tried with 1.108.44 which also does not work, then went back 1.136.2 and it works... that's a big bummer at the moment..

Regards,

Patrick

error=Source path: $.displayName is not available in content, but is required in defined system transformation Caused by: Source path: $.displayName is not available in content, but is required in defined system transformation Caused by: No results for path: $['displayName'],

content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça

entry.title: Re: Can we enforce "Move currently assigned Position belo...
entry.content: I wish SAP would come back with a solution here. Our company requires any replacement positions to go through workflow approval. The default toggle allows managers to transfer employees to another manager therefore creating new positions disguised as manager changes. For instance, a manager with 4 direct reports transfers their 4 employees to another manager. System creates 4 new positions under the new manager and keep 4 vacant positions under the current manager. So now there are 8 positions in total. We are looking to have the default toggle = Yes for managers but the code doesnt seem to exist

That's true, but just in case: 
On the other side of the street there is a very nice Falafel (Food) option to go or to sit.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Team Spotlight: Moshe Naveh - June 2025 Edition
entry.content: I’ve known Moshe since the old SCN times and had the chance to meet him in person. He’s always had a real talent for bringing people together in the community. Great interview!

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria
entry.content:
Nigeria’s Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) has launched its Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS)—a PEPPOL-aligned electronic invoicing platform set to revolutionize tax compliance. Rollout begins with large taxpayers in July 2025, extending to medium and small businesses soon after.

We are working directly with the FIRS team on our SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria Solution which is discussed below:

How It Works

  • ERP or API Integration: Businesses can integrate their existing Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems with the MBS via API connectors or middleware plugins. This integration ensures seamless transmission of invoices from back-end systems directly to the FIRS validation hub.
  • Invoice Generation & Validation:
    • For B2B/B2G transactions, invoices are submitted to FIRS before being shared with the buyer. The invoice undergoes validation and receives a unique IRN, CSID, and a QR codethat ensures authenticity and traceability.
    • For B2C invoices, the supplier issues the invoice immediately to the customer and must upload it to the MBS within 24 hours for reporting compliance.
    • Storage & Auditability: All validated invoices are stored in the MBS repository, where both suppliers and buyers, as well as the FIRS, can retrieve them for audit, reconciliation, or VAT verification. 

FIRS has given a number of use cases which leads to solution options for clients :

Core Use Cases & Capabilities

  • B2B / B2G Invoicing (Continuous Transaction Controls): This use case supports digital exchange of invoices between businesses or government agencies. Each invoice must be validated in real-time by the FIRS Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS) system, receiving a unique Invoice Reference Number (IRN), a Cryptographic Stamp (CSID), and a QR code for authentication.

In the above scenario's a business can choose the following:

1) Register their ERP as an access point and connect direct to the FIRS system; while this is possible during our discussions with FIRS this is not the prefered solution

2) Use a certified access point and connect your ERP to this as with other PEPPOL solution, one of FIRS goals is also to understand cross border invoice flows and by connecting to the PEPPOL network FIRS will then be reported on overseas customer invoices and overseas vendor invoices recieved via the access point.

  • Crimson-Partners Access Point is already registered in Nigeria as per our other solutions and can provide this for you
  • A company can choose their own access point and our solution will connect to this

B2C Invoicing (Near‑real‑time Reporting): Retail transactions involving consumers must be reported within 24 hours. These invoices are issued directly to customers and subsequently uploaded to the MBS platform for compliance.

  • Working with FIRS we have discussed using a consolidated invoice process daily to achieve this reporting for those with B2C sales

  • Standardized Formats & Access Points: Nigeria has adopted the UBL (Universal Business Language) XML standard to ensure interoperability. Invoices must be transmitted through licensed Access Point Providers (APPs) certified by NITDA. These APPs provide secure and traceable communication channels between senders and receivers via the PEPPOL 4-corner model. 

FIRS also provides an API to check the TIN validity of your customer before submitting invoicing accurately

✅Benefits for Nigerian Organizations

  • Improved Compliance: Real-time invoice validation eliminates the risks of underreporting or delayed VAT submissions.
  • Enhanced Transparency: The use of QR codes and digital cryptographic stamps ensures each invoice is traceable, tamper-proof, and verifiable by both buyer and tax authority.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automation reduces manual processing, lowers administrative overhead, and facilitates faster transaction processing.
  • Accurate VAT Reporting: Invoice data is automatically captured and structured for VAT compliance, reducing the need for manual declarations.

⚖ Legal Mandate & Penalties
The implementation of electronic invoicing is legally mandated by the FIRS under the Finance Act, supported by regulatory frameworks from NITDA and NCC. Non-compliance can lead to penalties including monetary fines, interest on underreported taxes, and restrictions on eligibility for input VAT claims.

� Rollout Strategy & Timeline

  • July 2025: Mandatory for large taxpayers as classified by FIRS.
  • Q4 2025: Gradual onboarding of medium-sized businesses.
  • 2026: Full rollout to small businesses and B2C operations.

Crimson provide a standard SAP Document Reporting Compliance Solution, a full staging area for testing, training and handbooks for your organisation

Benefits :

  • Single Global Process using Document Reporting Compliance
  • E2E compliant solution built with the FIRS team
  • Generation of electronic invoices in XML format (FI, SD) for B2B and B2G
  • Self-billing scenario's
  • FICA and RE-FX billing
  • Manual or automated invoice submission
  • Receiving of e-invoices in XML format and Integration to VIM Software
  • Human Readable PDF in Cockpit for incoming invoices

� Next Steps

  • Confirm your registration with FIRS and verify your Tax Identification Number (TIN).
  • Engage with a licensed Access Point Provider (APP) or Software Integrator (SI) to assist with system integration such as Crimson who can meet both these
  • Prepare your ERP systems or point-of-sale platforms for API connectivity and XML invoice generation.
  • Conduct end-to-end testing with FIRS and ensure your teams are trained in the e-invoicing process and legal requirements. 

� Bottom Line
The FIRSMBS e-invoicing system marks a major milestone in Nigeria’s digital tax transformation. It also marks a shift for African countries with other African nations choosing highly complex solutions, Nigeria has chosen a global standard and is following in similar fashion to Malaysia, UAE and other upcoming mandates.

It ensures:

  • Real-time transparency
  • Reduced invoicing errors
  • Automated VAT reporting
  • Lower compliance costs

Compatible with ECC, S/4HANA & S/4 Cloud, this is another step on the journey of global electronic invoicing across borders.

Look out next for our Kenya, Zambia, Ghana solution and BTP Application!

Crimson Tax Tech | FIRS e-Invoicing Advisory

Suhaib

entry.title: Is there a SAP Module that is able to maintain lab equipment outside the plant maintenance area?
entry.content: We are looking for a way to maintain quality lab equipment, calibrations etc that is not planned/maintained by a Maintenance Planner.  Meaning the Quality Associates would need to plan when service vendors arrive on site to calibrate scales specifically located in their lab area.  How is this area maintained if Plant Maintenance will not pick up that work?  thank you,

entry.title: Restricting Access at Storage Location Level in IM & EWM
entry.content:
Hi,

In our current SAP S/4HANA setup, user access is controlled at the plant level for Inventory Management (IM) and at the warehouse level for Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

We are exploring the possibility of implementing more granular restrictions at the storage location level within both IM and EWM modules. Has anyone implemented such restrictions, or is there a recommended approach (e.g., using authorizations, enhancements, or config settings) to achieve this?

Appreciate your insights or experiences!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Create just Purchase Requisition for Stock Material f...
entry.content:
In the standard configuration using the PFV proc. ind. is Non-Stock and generates a PR and no reservation.  I copied PFV and made it L, which resulted in a reservation and no PR.  

Please show your config to get the results you wanted.   And what is your business process to consume the inventory?

entry.title: Re: Joule answers in eclipse
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for the answer. In some tests it got better with the sentence. But especially when programming, I never want to have invented certain specifications. In other words, I expected that one of the advantages of Joule over other llm's is that I don't have to specify such sets. Is it possible to set this as a default? Or even better, ATC variants according to which it should be based?

Best regards

Stefan

entry.title: How can I execute iRPA automation from the build App
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm quite new to the iRPA with Build Process Automation.  I am hoping I can get some guidance here.  I have built an automation that can read excel file and execute a transaction in the backend.  It's working fine when testing the automation directly in BPA.  I have 3 input parameters in this automation.  My question is, how can an end user uses this automation, manually input the value and execute the automation?  My initial thought was to use BuildApp as a wrapper app and trigger the automation.  However, I have tried to configure the Destination, publish the automation, created a frame in build App with input fields.  However, I couldn't find the automation (process) in the published library, and hence, cannot link the build app with automation.  I am not sure if there is anything I've done incorrectly , or is there other solution ?  I'd appreciate any guidance/insights.  Thanks in advance !!

entry.title: Re: SAP CI-DS in SAP BTP Cloud foundry
entry.content:
Hi Malathi

Currently all CPI-DS servers are hosted on the Neo environment, where these will all be migrated to the Kyma environment in the future (see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration-for-data-services/tenant-migration-guide/link-migration-waves).

There is no option to have the CPI-DS servers/tenants hosted on other environments.

Regards,
Ian
SAP Support

entry.title: Failed to load UI5 component for navigation intent "#SalesOrder-manageV2"
SAP CRM – Scope Filter Not Working Until Page Refresh (User-Specific Issue)
entry.content:
Hi all,

i'm facing a strange issue in SAP CRM WebUI, specifically in the Request for Change (RfC) screen within the Scope tab.

 

  • When a specific user opens a Request for Change and tries to use the Scope filter, it doesn't return any results initially.

  • However, once the user refreshes the page manually (F5) and tries the same filter again, it starts working properly.

  • From that point on, the filter continues to work as expected during the rest of the session.

  • Other users do not experience this problem.What i tried is as follow:

  • Cleared browser cache
  • Checked user roles/authorizations (seem fine)
  • Tried with different browsers (same behavior).

Has anyone encountered a similar filter or refresh-specific issue in the CRM WebUI or have an idea about this issue.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Could not play Bite size videos on CPQ - Configure Price Quote
entry.content: Hi Sergey, can you share the error you are getting?

entry.title: Re: Failed to load UI5 component for navigation intent "#SalesOrder-manageV2"
entry.content: more

entry.title: Re: Issue in developing GSTR2 report
entry.content:
Hello @sapcomyogik 

This is a common issue when developing tax reports in S/4HANA Public Cloud, especially for GSTR2, due to the different data models used in

  • I_JournalEntryItem contains general ledger items, including tax lines, but tax amounts can be merged or distributed based on document structure.
  • I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem is designed to extract tax-specific information (e.g., Tax Codes, Tax Base, Tax Amount) in alignment with transactional tax reporting requirements like GSTR2.

Recommendation

Use the Journal Entry Header (I_JournalEntry) as your primary link between both CDS Views via the JournalEntry and CompanyCode fields.

  • Ensure you join I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem based on JournalEntry and CompanyCode, then map tax amounts by TaxCode, TaxBaseAmount, or TaxAmount.
  • Keep in mind that not all tax amounts shown in accounting lines (I_JournalEntryItem) will directly match tax amounts in the tax-specific CDS unless aligned through correct joins and aggregation logic.

SAP Help references-Tax Reporting and CDS View Documentation

For accurate GSTR2 reporting,SAP recommended to build your logic primarily from I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem, as this view reflects tax amounts from the operational perspective, ensuring better alignment with statutory reports.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Hi @mlauber ,

Thanks for sharing this.

I tried to reproduce in my S/42023OP(FPS01) but no triggering events. Same behavior with businesspartnerTP and purchaseOrderTP.

Inbound destination is configured in SBGRFCCONF. Seems there are no other prerequisites specified by SAP documentation.

So I have the impression that either it's not functional on a S/42023OP(FPS1), or there's another config point missing other than SBGRFCCONF, or I don't know 😀. I would really try to use local events to switch from old way.

If anyone has any ideas, tips or advice, I'll take them all.

 

Br,

Samy

entry.title: Re: EDI Setup for Billing Document - S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

Given API is related to SAP_COM_0440 - Warehouse Stock Transfer Receipt Advice – Receive from Warehouse .

its not related to billing , We have used SAP_COM_0120, but communication was not established.

any help w.r.t to the API .

Regards,

Asesh Kumar

entry.title: AfO exception cell format change
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I am trying to change the format for exception cells in AfO.

The standard format from SAP would be red bold font on a reddish background.

When using this, I get the reddish background, but a black font.

We do not want to have the reddish background, but the red bold font.

Now whatever I do in the format settings - for the exception cell it does not use the font setting, no red, green or whatsoever.

Funnily enough, for eg the readonlycell I can choose a red font without issues.

 

Is this a known issue? can I not change the font color for exception cell? 

What do I need to do?

Thanks

Cornelia

entry.title: Re: Log of changes in Material Master EWM
entry.content:
Good morning, in the same transaction /N/SCWM/MAT1, you can find the user who last modified the product.

I haven't found any other view or transaction that shows you the history.

Regards.

EWM - Master Data 

SAP enhancement package for SAP ERP, version for SAP HANA

entry.title: Re: PCE edition EPIC
entry.content:
Hello @Sunny10 

In most cases, EPIC requires an extra licence under Advanced Cash Management for PCE. Best to check your existing contract or consult your SAP Account Executive for confirmation.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Puzzle Challenge in SAP
entry.content: I wrote these puzzles many years ago. Last week, I tried a few of them, but I couldn't quite recall how to solve them. Unfortunately, I didn't keep the draft of solution for all of them. Anyway, this makes it more challenging even for myself. Have fun, if anyone really need a solution for any puzzle, feel free to email me via chenhansen(AT)hotmail.com

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Events in Warsaw - 2025
entry.content: Could you pls add the link to submit talk proposals for SIT?

entry.title: Re: Conversion of XML to JSON using Generic Java Mapping
entry.content:
Hello,

we are able to convert XML to JSON and it is working fine. But the ordering of fields is changing. can you please let us know how to achieve this. For me the field sequence mandatory at target side.

XML Data:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ns0:MT_WbsMasterData xmlns:ns0="urn:Kalpataru-xxx.com:xxxx:I001:WbsMasterData">
<batch_request_id/>
<batch_update_url/>
<operations>
<body>
<data>
<user_id/>
<metafields>
<WBSElement/>
<SiteName/>
<PlantCode/>
<PlantDescription/>
<BusinessPlace/>
<BusinessPlaceDescription/>
<Region/>
<Input1/>
<Input2/>
<Input3/>
<Input4/>
<Input5/>
</metafields>
</data>
<requestId/>
<container_type/>
<product_name/>
</body>
<requestId/>
<method/>
<path/>
<headers>
<name/>
<value/>
</headers>
</operations>
</ns0:MT_WbsMasterData>

JSON Data:

{
"operations": {
"path": "",
"headers": {
"name": "",
"value": ""
},
"method": "",
"requestId": "",
"body": {
"data": {
"metafields": {
"BusinessPlace": "",
"WBSElement": "",
"Input1": "",
"SiteName": "",
"Region": "",
"PlantDescription": "",
"Input2": "",
"PlantCode": "",
"Input3": "",
"BusinessPlaceDescription": "",
"Input4": "",
"Input5": ""
},
"user_id": ""
},
"requestId": "",
"container_type": "",
"product_name": ""
}
},
"batch_request_id": "",
"batch_update_url": ""
}
}

For my scenario batch_request_id and batch_update_url needs to be there in top on nodes but while converting XML to JSON it is adding in bottom of the JSON data.  I need the JSON data same as my source xml after converting.

Can any one help me how to achieve field sequence in this case.

Regards,

Ashok Kumar.

entry.title: Re: New hire Inactive in header but Job Information is Ac...
entry.content:
Hi Joana, 

I am facing the same issue. Even after the HRIS sync has run, the employee status does not get synced from Job Info to EP. In this case the employee has hire date as of yesterday. It happened in the past for other users and my conclusion was that it takes time, since on the day after the user was already active. Hopefully we will find a more logical reason soon.

entry.title: Re: Quick reference for Variable Pay Individual View and Enhanced Variable Pay Individual View
entry.content: Thank you for the informative post.

entry.title: Re: New Employee Inactive Profile but Job Information is...
entry.content: Hello, I am facing the same for a user with a hire date as of yesterday and the HRIS sync already run. Shouldn't this be enough to make it active in EP?

entry.title: Re: Document type field modification (BSART) in the purch...
entry.content: The order type and numbering range are the same. And only for category D it does not allow changes.

entry.title: Re: Extra line item after batch determination in Process order
entry.content: This is an annoying problem, it also makes another problem in material quantity calculations for API filler

entry.title: Re: Create just Purchase Requisition for Stock Material f...
entry.content:
Hi Ken. Your “standard” configuration is not “standard.” LOL It has been modified.

It doesn’t make sense to make an advance purchase when, in theory, you don’t know at which point in production the material will be used, and already define which object will receive the accounting. In this case, it wouldn’t be an advance purchase; it would just be a purchase of non-stock material. Check in other environments, because your “standard” configuration has definitely been modified.

entry.title: Re: Mastering Day 2 Operations in SAP BTP Webcast Series
entry.content: All the sessions were super helpful, thank you for all the speakers for providing their valuable insights about the topic !!

entry.title: Re: Create just Purchase Requisition for Stock Material f...
entry.content: Please show your standard PFV configuration.

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to delete fs item
entry.content: @ArnoldWarhonowicz , check this note and implemet : 3458654 - Clean up of Sel FS items (C/I) not Filtering Properly.

entry.title: Re: Cumulative/YTD load for FI to Group Reporting Integra...
entry.content:
@Eyal_Feiler  I agree with your observation—while ECCS offers various options, it's important to note that SAP Group Reporting (GR) is a periodic system where data is stored in periodic mode.

As you rightly pointed out, GRDC is currently out of scope and not preferred. However, we would still recommend utilizing GRDC as an interim solution until the 2025 upgrade.

Post-upgrade, you will have access to the Release Universal Journal – Support for Prior Periods functionality, which enables both full and delta mode processing for earlier periods.

Alternatively, you may also consider leveraging the standard API or the flexible upload functionality, based on your system landscape and data integration needs.

Otheroption : release the data every period and run the consolidation scenarios once in a year P12/P16.   I don't recommend the GRPL- YTD realignment. this will blast the volumes in the Universal Journal.

entry.title: Re: build error on Build apps runtime 4.15.24
entry.content: Same problem with updated runtime 4.15.26

entry.title: Integration with Third Party PLM Systems
entry.content:
In our first blog, Unify, Innovate, Excel, we introduced SAP’s strategic vision for Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) — a unified, integrated approach to manage product data, processes, and collaboration across the enterprise. In our second blog, we explored how this strategy plays out in discrete industries, focusing on the tight integration between engineering tools, BOM transitions, and cross-functional collaboration.  In our third blog, we shifted our focus on how our strategy for process industries addresses their unique challenges in managing recipe data, ensuring compliance, enabling collaboration, and transitioning smoothly from development to execution. We will now wrap up our discussion with the last blog in our series, turning our focus towards those customers who use external PLM solutions to manage their product development processes with the need to smoothly transition product data from development to execution to establish an integrated digital thread for the enterprise.

Understanding the PLM Ecosystem

Product Lifecycle Management is not a new concept; companies have been looking for ways to continuously streamline their design to operate processes while software providers have been delivering solutions to meet these customer needs. As a result, there are customers who have taken a decision to invest in external PLM systems like Siemens Teamcenter, Dassault Systèmes 3DEXPERIENCE, PTC Windchill or Autodesk Vault to store product design details coming from various design authoring tools. Adding to the complexity of the situation, the same customer may have more than one of these solutions in place, depending upon the given industry (aerospace and defense, for example) and a customer’s acquisition history. In the end, all these customers have the need to have their product design information seamlessly flow from these external PLM tools into the ERP environments to streamline supply chain process.

SAP is well-equipped to support customers with a reliable, standards-based integration solution that centralizes product data within the SAP Business Suite. This facilitates efficient data sharing across the value chain and enables effective bi-directional change management for organizations.

As a result, customers can preserve their existing investments in their PLM technology, reduce their total cost of ownership with out-of-the-box integration, and leverage additional SAP capabilities to accommodate hybrid scenarios like supplier collaboration and visualization, tailored to their business needs.

Let’s briefly look at a few key areas on how SAP’s solution, known as PLM system integration, enables interoperation between SAP and external PLM systems to enable efficient product data flows and better collaboration.

Efficient Pipeline for Product Data

First and foremost, it is a necessity that any successful integration provides the ability to exchange data between systems. SAP’s goal is not only to provide this but ultimately to boost efficiency and collaboration within an organization with bi-directional integrations for unified product data across the entire supply chain.

When considering data, what needs to be passed is not just limited to parts, and bills of materials, but also needs to include additional product relevant data necessary for change management, variant definitions, vendor data, production operations, inspection characteristics and others.  When the breadth of the data is expanded to include all these facets, it becomes possible to support a wide range of business process scenarios (MTS, MTO, ETO, CTO, CTO+) with the product data.

PLM system integration not only handles these data needs, but it does so by using the taxonomies used to describe product data in the different departments within a company.   For example, the solution understands that in engineering an "object" might be called a part or an item, but in SAP the manufacturing and purchasing understand that same "object" as a material master.   Having this common taxonomy framework in place ensures that enabling the integration is not delayed due to communication issues.

PLM system integration performs the data exchange between SAP and the external PLM systems, through highly performant and automatic process triggers - meaning data in passed in context and when it is required.  When data is transferred and handled in the proper sequence (for example, in SAP materials need to be created before adding to them to a bill of material), we ensure a unified and complete view of the data is available in both SAP and the external PLM system.  This improves data quality and transparency across the entire supply chain.

End to End Business Process Integration

While seamlessly transferring data is important, what drives true value for customers is the ability to integrate PLM systems such as Teamcenter, 3DEXPERIENCE, PTC Windchill, and Autodesk Vault to support end-to-end business processes.

What do we mean by end-to-end business processes? As mentioned above, customers need to support a wide range of business process scenarios (MTS, MTO, ETO, CTO, CTO+). When considering the different needs of these processes, PLM system integration supports the integration of data within the context of end to end business processes (Supported Business Processes).

To enable this, PLM system integration comes with standard content in the form of Business Configuration Sets to enable the easy integration of SAP to an external PLM system.   You can use this content for the initial configuration of your SAP system, and you can change and extend the configuration wherever necessary, to fit your needs.  

Efficient and Transparent Data Management 

We discussed this topic during our second blog, but it is worth mentioning again in the context of bringing product data into SAP via PLM system integration.  There is always the need for product design information to be made available to the supply chain for downstream processes.   For example, manufacturing needs a manufacturing bill of material (mBOM) to support production operations, but engineering only provides their view of the product in the form of an engineering bill of material (eBOM).  

Integrating external PLM systems with SAP simplifies the transition from eBOM to mBOM by seamlessly transferring engineering data from an external PLM system into SAP. SAP’s capabilities can then convert the eBOM to mBOM while keeping structures aligned and under change control. Additionally, visualization data linked to business data can be easily created, supporting downstream processes like service management, asset repair, and spare parts ordering. These are opportunities to boost production readiness, reduce rework, and improve efficiency.

By building a seamless connection between engineering and other departments—such as manufacturing, sales, sourcing, or service— we improve product quality, maintain consistency amid changes, and enable the reuse of engineering data for the enterprise. This positions us to bring new products to market more swiftly.

What does the future hold?

While the future is hard to predict, we know the one constant in the world is change.  As customer processes change and as industries continue to evolve, SAP is poised to help navigate the challenges and opportunities ahead. By continuing to optimize and enhance the capabilities provided for integration to external PLM tools, SAP addresses both current industry needs and future demands.

With the focus on seamlessly integrating product data across engineering, sourcing, manufacturing, quality, service, etc., SAP helps deliver an integrated digital thread across the organization where not only is the product data integrated, but the processes are integrated as well.   This empowers organizations to reduce errors, improve agility, and meet the growing need for sustainable, high-quality products, not only now, but in the future as well!

To learn more about our strategy, solution capabilities, and future innovations for PLM system integration, please visit the following links: 

  • Strategy blog: SAP Business Suite – The Integrated Powerhouse for Seamless Product Lifecycle Management
  • Strategy blog: Collaborative Product Development in Discrete Industries
  • Strategy blog: Recipe Formulation and Specification Management in Process Industries
  • SAP website: Solutions for product lifecycle management 
  • SAP Road Map Explorer: Innovations for PLM system integration

Marcelo Romeiro

entry.title: How to retain archive deletes in SLT target
entry.content:
We are using SLT to replicate tables data from SAP ECC.  We will be performing archiving in SAP ECC.  We want to keep the archived data in HANA. What is the SAP recommended solution to keep deleted rows due to archiving in source SAP ECC system from being deleted in HANA?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to retain archive deletes in SLT target
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Please see KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2821492

 

Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Modify SFTP Report
entry.content:
Good afternoon, everyone!

I have a question concerning an FTP report configuration that we have set up in our LMS. We have a User Item Status CSV report that is being exported to one of our teams. However, the report is exporting the item status for all items in our LMS. The team that receives this report is requesting that the export file only include information for specific items/courses, and that the information is sent is a specific format. Is there a way to modify the FTP configuration to only export the information for a specific set of items/courses? Or will we have to stop the FTP export of the report and manually schedule a recurring report with only the specific items/courses? I hope my question makes sense.

Below is the report system configuration that we currently have. This is what I am wanting to modify to only pull specific items. Is this possible?

# for a specific report, add
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.protocol=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.server=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.port=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.path=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.userI
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.password=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.timeout=
reportFtpConfig.UserItemStatusCSV.enablePassiveMode=

entry.title: Re: How to delete/remove SM58 TRFC failed entries using transaction ID
entry.content: How to delete bulk RFC errors? Its difficult to delete one after the other RFC's

entry.title: Custo Padrão
entry.content:
Olá, boa tarde!

Sou recém-chegado à comunidade SAP.

Fizemos recentemente a migração do SAP ECC para o SAP S/4 HANA.

Na versão ECC o custo padrão gerado mensalmente era sofria alteração, creio eu, ser em função da movimentação do material durante o período.

Já na versão S/4 Hana, o custo padrão não está mais sofrendo alteração, desde janeiro, quando foi feito a virada de sistema, o custo padrão permanece o mesmo valor.

Alguém saberia esclarecer essa questão?

Desde já, obrigado.

entry.title: S4/HANA - How to develop an SAPUI5 app calling a backend logic
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I want to know if there's any way to develop an app that simulates a screen, just like we had in ECC, with selection criteria.

The idea is to have a class developed in the back end and then have an app developed in SAPUI5 that calls it, without necessarily needing an OData service.

Kind Regards,

Marcelo Romeiro

entry.title: Getting error while building Build Apps runtime 4.15.24 and 4.15.26
entry.content:
Hi Every one. 
I'm struggling trying to build my app. It was fully functional. Last success build was april 16. Now I did some minor changes and I'm getting error from the builder.  Please see the log.  Anyone else dealing with this?

I've tried to build a previously successfully built past version. Got same error this time.

In preview everything is working perfectly.

Hope anyone can help me.  


Build Log
AB version: 3.30.1
COMMAND: 

USER_METERING_ENABLED=true SHOW_SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION=false SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION_DISMISSIBLE=true NEXT_SUPPORT_RELATIVE_PATHS=true NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=2000 --no-experimental-global-navigator  npxrnv export -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --packageManager yarn 

FAILED with ERROR: 

warn: You have integration @sba-runtime/integration-builder defined, but it wasn't found in package.json. ERR: Error: Cannot find module '../renative.json'
Require stack:
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/config.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/tasks/taskExport.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/index.js
- ./node_modules/@rnv/core/lib/modules/index.js
Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/app/.appgyver/app/renative.private.json'
 at Object.openSync (node:fs:561:18)
 at Object.readFileSync (node:fs:445:35)
 at handleCryptoApiKeys (/app/current/buildHooks/dist/index.js:29381:56)
 at /app/current/node_modules/@rnv/core/src/buildHooks/index.ts:21:50
 at Object.dynatraceRegularInvoke [as doInvoke] (/opt/dynatrace/oneagent-paas/agent/bin/1.311.79.20250515-130846/any/nodejs/nodejsagent.js:4087:22)
error: ⨯ ○ export: COMMAND: 
node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build 
FAILED with ERROR: 
> Build failed because of webpack errors
Error: Command failed with exit code 1: node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build
 ⚠ Invalid next.config.js options detected: 
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected undefined, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.appSwitcherTests"
> Build failed because of webpack errors
Attention: Next.js now collects completely anonymous telemetry regarding usage.
This information is used to shape Next.js' roadmap and prioritize features.
You can learn more, including how to opt-out if you'd not like to participate in this anonymous program, by visiting the following URL:
https://nextjs.org/telemetry

 at handlePromise (/app/current/node_modules/execa/index.js:118:26)

 ⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
> Build failed because of webpack errors
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

entry.title: Re: Modify SFTP Report
entry.content: @Ivy_McGruder, if I understand it correctly, you'll need to stop the current recurring report job and schedule a new one, applying filters to include only the desired courses. This can't be done through the "REPORT_SYSTEM" configuration.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @sam_venkat,

Did you test via the api.sap.com ? But normally in the json, you should have [urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User][userUuid]

Regards

entry.title: Re: Vendor Consignment stock ( S4 Hana Public Cloud )
entry.content: The consignment purchasing info record needs to be created with tax code and valid price before the consignment settlement program can work.  MRKO uses the PIR price, not PO price.

entry.title: Re: Custom Report on SF LMS exam Data Using API – Include...
entry.content:
@siddhesh_chavan, in Story, for an assessment exam, you can try the following tables and columns in the query designer:

  • "Exam" table: "Quiz ID" column >  
  • "Exam Objective" table: "Objective" and "Objective ID" column >
  • "Question" table: "Question ID" column >
  • "Question Variant" table >
  • "Question Version" table: "Stem" column >
  • "Answer I18N" table: "Choice Value".

entry.title: Re: Questionmark Secure for SAP Certification - how to test
entry.content: test directly from when you are scheduling your exam, there is an option to detect questionnaire software , it does not work directly means you cannot test using your window app, cheers !!

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Conversion
entry.content: Extremely helpful in terms of working and understanding overall project level. Thanks for the blog. Really helpful.

entry.title: Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model: Gaining Traction & Leveraging Visibility at Level 2 (3 of 5)
entry.content:
For companies that have moved beyond purely manual processes and have implemented a basic transportation management system (TMS), the Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model places them around Level 2, "Somewhat Digitalized". At this level, you have core capabilities like load consolidation and booking management in place, but with limited point-to-point connections with a few partners, likely via traditional EDI. Nonetheless, communication often still defaults to emails and phone calls, especially when circumstances deviate from plans and annual  contracts. Visibility into routes, rates, shipment status, and up-to-date ETAs are available, but often require logging into and navigating through multiple external portals for each carrier, separate from your core order management systems and fulfillment processes.

Companies at Level 2 have begun their digital journey, but key limitations remain:

  • TMS processes cover load consolidation, tendering, and booking with limited carrier collaboration.
  • Limited visibility into shipment status and risk derived from multiple external portals disconnected from fulfillment.
  • Some cross-functional integration and proactivity but gaps preventing process optimization and digital collaboration.

At this stage, you have some pieces of the puzzle digitalized, but they aren't fully connected, and therefore fail to provide the comprehensive, real-time insights needed for truly proactive decision-making.  For this reason, the primary focus for improvement shifts to leveraging logistics visibility and process integration to improve agility.

Level 2 companies must focus on connecting more trading partners digitally, while maintaining efficiency at scale, and integrating visibility data directly into their logistics systems as well as the order management processes of their ERP. Moving beyond limited point-to-point connections to a business network becomes essential for scalable collaboration. This enhanced connectivity enables more near real-time data exchange for processes like tendering, ordering, and tracking, allowing companies to avoid chasing email and stopping to log into external portals. By integrating visibility into internal systems, you can start identifying issues in the context of your business, and addressing them along your embedded processes before they become major disruptions, setting the stage for greater resilience.

Return for the next installment that dives into how companies at Level 3 build on this foundation to become truly connected and proactive.   (prior post in this series) 

Read more about logistics visibility:

What is logistics visibility?

Inbound Logistics magazine: 10 Tips to Effective Logistics Visibility

Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace over individual visibility platforms?

               Brief

               SAP Community post series

Product Tour: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

entry.title: I always had one doubt in my mind that how people are so dramatic about marketing
entry.content:
What I love must about marketing is that I get to make people fall in love with brands.

I remember being on one of my first college classes, it was “Introduction to marketing or something like that, can’t quite remember. The point is that our teacher was just explaining what marketers job really is.

Her statement went as follows: “You know how doctors heal people, architects design buildings and houses? Well, us marketers, make people fall in love with brands”

Those words stuck in my head from that moment back in 2011. They made me love marketing from that moment on.

I get to work with amazing brands and people. I see how much effort and love people put into creating a brand, I get to help them not only get their brand (something they feel so proud of) out there but also help them grow their business. There is not a more rewarding feeling than seeing someone succeed through your help.

entry.title: Error creating workarea in SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi, I'm having a problem with my Enable Now tenant.
When I try to create a Workarea, I get the following error.

 

Similarly, when trying to give permissions to a user within an existing workarea, I get the following error

I have the administrator role and all the permissions to create workareas, but I don't know what the error is. Can you guide me?

Thanks

entry.title: Integration with Vonage
entry.content:
Hi,

Does anyone have experience with connecting SBO to Vonage service for outbound calling?

entry.title: Architecture Gone Wrong: Leveraging SAP Architecture Center for Better Outcomes
entry.content:
In the today growing software development, architecture plays a crucial role in determining the success and efficiency of a system. Poor architecture can lead to significant costs, performance issues, and maintenance headaches.

This blog explores the pitfalls of bad architecture and highlights the advantages of leveraging the SAP Architecture Center to ensure best practices and cost savings.

The Pitfalls of Poor Architecture

1. High Costs: Poorly designed architecture can lead to increased costs in several ways:

  • Maintenance: Systems with bad architecture require frequent fixes and updates, leading to higher maintenance costs.
  • Scalability: Inefficient architecture can struggle to scale, necessitating expensive overhauls or replacements.
  • Performance: Suboptimal architecture can result in slow performance, requiring additional resources to meet performance standards.

2. Performance Issues: Bad architecture can severely impact system performance:

  • Latency: Inefficient data flow and processing can lead to high latency, affecting user experience.
  • Reliability: Poor architecture can result in frequent downtimes and failures, reducing system reliability.
  • Resource Utilization: Inefficient use of resources can lead to higher operational costs and lower performance.

The SAP Architecture Center: A Solution

The SAP Architecture Center offers a comprehensive suite of tools, best practices, and reference architectures to help businesses design efficient and cost-effective systems

https://architecture.learning.sap.com 

Here are some key benefits:

1. Best Practices: The SAP Architecture Center provides access to industry best practices, ensuring that your architecture is designed to meet the highest standards. This includes guidelines for security, performance, and scalability.

2. Reference Architectures: Reference architectures are templates that outline the interactions between various services and components. They provide a standardized approach to designing systems, ensuring optimal configurations and seamless integration 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchCatalog/ 

3. Community-Driven Guidance: The SAP Architecture Center fosters a community of practice where architects and developers can share insights, collaborate, and learn from each other. This collaborative environment helps in adopting proven frameworks and innovative solutions

https://architecture.learning.sap.com/community/intro 

Advantages and Benefits

1. Cost Savings: By following SAP best practices and utilizing reference architectures, businesses can significantly reduce costs:

  • Reduced Maintenance: Well-designed architecture requires less frequent fixes and updates.
  • Efficient Resource Utilization: Optimal configurations ensure efficient use of resources, reducing operational costs.
  • Scalability: Scalable architecture minimizes the need for expensive overhauls.

2. Improved Performance: Leveraging the SAP Architecture Center can lead to better system performance:

  • Lower Latency: Efficient data flow and processing reduce latency.
  • Higher Reliability: Robust architecture ensures fewer downtimes and failures.
  • Optimal Resource Utilization: Efficient use of resources leads to better performance.

FinOps for Cost Saving

Financial Operations (FinOps) is a practice that combines financial management with cloud operations to optimize costs. Integrating FinOps with SAP best practices can further enhance cost savings:

  1. Cost Monitoring: FinOps tools help in monitoring cloud spending, identifying cost drivers, and optimizing resource usage.
  2. Budgeting and Forecasting: FinOps practices enable accurate budgeting and forecasting, ensuring that financial resources are allocated efficiently.
  3. Continuous Improvement: FinOps encourages continuous improvement by regularly reviewing and optimizing cloud spending, leading to sustained cost savings.

Conclusion

Poor architecture can lead to significant costs and performance issues. However, by leveraging the SAP Architecture Center and adopting FinOps practices, businesses can design efficient, cost-effective, and high-performing systems. The SAP Architecture Center provides best practices, reference architectures, and community-driven guidance to ensure that your architecture is built to the highest standards. Embrace these tools and practices to avoid the pitfalls of bad architecture and achieve optimal results.

entry.title: Re: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content:
It looks like you are passing the values of the custom fields as offset data in the VALUEPART1 field of the EXTENSIONIN parameter.

If so, I believe you would need to copy those values from this structure into matching fields of EKPO using a badi implementation.

Take a look at the method EXTENSIONIN of the badi ME_BAPI_PO_IBS. I think you need to fill your custom fields in POITEM as the data from function module BAPI_PO_CHANGE is processed.

Did you add your custom fields to structure MEPOITEM as well as to EKPO?

entry.title: Re: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content: Method EXTENSIONIN of Badi ME_BAPI_PO_CUST might be a better choice.

entry.title: Re: AfO exception cell format change
entry.content: First of all, are you using the latest version of AfO to eliminate old errors?

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Validate And Enrich with SAP BTP Integration Suite – Part 1
entry.content:
Nice blog. Step by step information. 

Thanks for sharing

entry.title: Setup of Print Queues in OPD Transaction
entry.content: I'm trying to setup some printers in the OPD transaction where the printers are of type Q as defined in SPAD and point to CPM queues. In OPD however I'm not able to see/select those printers as valid. I only see printers defined as type 'L' in SPAD. How do I configure so I can point to my CPM printers?

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA - How to develop an SAPUI5 app calling a back...
entry.content:
By definition, a SAPUI5 app is a web app so it runs in the browser, regardless if’s consuming data from S/4HANA, ECC, CRM or any other SAP or non SAP system. 
as the app runs in user’s browser, you cannot select data like in an classic ABAP application (I guess this is what you mean by “ECC” in your question).

so, you need a REST API - regardless it’s OData or not. By if your backend is an S/4HANA system creating an API using ABAP (Using RAP for recent versions specially) is the easiest and default choice

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content: @FlorentTardivon Not tested with api.sap.com.  In Azure, don't see the IAS attribute for userUuid listed . Added it manually and tested, does not work.

entry.title: LMS Data Story Report
entry.content:
I am trying to create a story report based on LMS data that shows the total amount of time spent in courses by our employees. I am summing on the duration field. However, many employees have been assigned a course multiple times, despite completing it only once so the duration is being overstated when added. 

For example, I have records like:

Employee#Course NameAssignment DateCompletion DateDuration
1234Course ABCJan. 1, 2025May 31, 202510
1234 Course ABCFeb. 15, 2025May 31, 202510
1234Course ABCMar. 15, 2025May 31, 202510

In the above example, the employee completed the course once and so I want to show the duration as 10, not 30. Does anyone know of a way of accomplishing this? Is there a way to filter the query or the table on the max of completion date for the same employee id and course name?

entry.title: Suggestions to execute mass stock relocation without manually creating a WT for each item
entry.content:
We are relocating stock within the same warehouse. The relocation involves new storage types and bins but within the same warehouse number.

We want to:

Physically move all materials from old bins to new ones

Record article numbers, quantities, base unit of measure, and new vs old storage bins

Avoid creating individual warehouse tasks manually for each article

We are seeking guidance on the following:

Which SAP Fiori application should we use in S/4HANA Cloud to perform this kind of mass stock transfer?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Suggestions to execute mass stock relocation without...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In addition to the Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/mass-stock-relocation-in-warehouse-without-creating-individual-warehouse/qaq-p/14132251

I would recommend checking the ‘Transfer Stock – In-Plant’ app provides functionality for transferring stock between storage locations or bins within the same plant. It offers a guided process where the user can select materials, specify source and target storage locations, and confirm quantities to be transferred. 

Also, I would recommend checking the ‘Post Goods Movement’ app, that allows users to perform a wide range of goods movements, including goods receipts, issues, and transfer postings. It offers flexibility for different movement types and is often used in processes that involve adjustments, consumption postings, or more complex movement scenarios. 

However, neither app provides standard functionality to import an Excel file and automatically process multiple stock transfers. For bulk processing, additional development or automation would be necessary.

In addition, 

For more information:

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant (SAP Fiori Apps Library)

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant (SAP Help Portal)

  • Post Goods Movement (SAP Fiori Apps Library)

  • Post Goods Movement (SAP Help Portal)

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.
